findings.evidence: `Cylinder.lean:128` adds `[IsFiniteMeasure μ]`; Mathlib has `integral_indicator₀` at `Bochner/Set.lean:185` and `setIntegral_const` at `Bochner/Set.lean:527` without finite-measure assumptions, while `blockLaw` is just `μ.map` at `Basic.lean:37` and `blockLaw_apply_rectangle` at `Basic.lean:104` also has no `[IsFiniteMeasure μ]`.
findings.fix: Drop `[IsFiniteMeasure μ]` from `integral_blockIndicatorProd`; keep the existing finite-measure hypothesis only on `integrable_blockIndicatorProd`, where it proves actual integrability.
findings.issue: `integral_blockIndicatorProd` assumes `[IsFiniteMeasure μ]`, but the statement only relates a Bochner integral to `(blockLaw ...).toReal`; the proof uses `integral_indicator₀`, `setIntegral_const`, and `blockLaw_blockCylinder`, none of which require finite measure.

summary: The new lemma is stated with a finite-measure hypothesis that is stronger than the APIs it uses and stronger than the natural `toReal` statement.
